title: Tojis role in the Rebuild movies
display_title: Tojis role in the Rebuild movies
wikitext:
[]: Toji's role in the Rebuild movies (SPOILERS)             Quote Post by Zeruel » Mon Mar 22, 2010 7:51 pm Sense he obviously isn't the pilot of The Eva we thought he was do you think he will be the pilot of Unit 8 because I thought in the translation for 3 they say "he or him" describing pilot and unit 8. Or am i wrong? And if I am wrong do you think we will see Toji pilot any Eva in the last 2 movies.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Mar 22, 2010 8:14 pm Anything can really happen with the characters at this point. Due to the events of the final scene it is possible that the citizens of Tokyo-3 will evacuate/leave the city, Toji amongst those leaving. He could be the pilot of either Unit-07 or Unit-08. He could get killed in a fight against an angel. He could have been one of the victims of the fight against Zuruel. Or he will remain a supporting and/or be used as comic relief (assuming the possibility of any comedic moments in the remaining films).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 22, 2010 8:17 pm Actually, there's always a possibly he along with Hikari and Kensuke were killed off in the 10th Angel's attack (remember how he protected Hikari from the debris falling?) instead of being able to evacuate, but that's too depressing to think about. (Although it would be fitting, since episode 19 showed that infamous example of civilian casualties from the battles.) Only time will tell if Toji has some other role such as becoming a pilot though.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Mar 22, 2010 8:46 pm The whole class gang- Toji, Kensuke, and Hikari don't serve a point in the story anymore. I would've loved it if Anno had killed them off in the battle at the end of 2.0 (only because it would be so DARK) but they're alive. Otherwise he would've shown it. Regardless I figure they'll get one scene in 3.0 (if they're playing the two "films" back to back in the theaters, in my opinion that makes it one movie). The way I see it is all the survivors have been evacuated to shelters outside the city, Toji creates a scene wanting to know what's happened to Shinji, a random armed NERV/SEELE guy threatens him to keep quiet and not cause a panic, Kensuke & Hikari (and I guess the Little Sister) pull him back telling him to be quiet. Then Toji says some short line about Shinji and a drastic Anno cut to whatever madness is happening to Shinji & Rei in Unit 01. Literally 60 seconds top. Maybe a shot in the climax watching whatever godlike insanity occurs. If it's happy ending, they're cheering, if it's dark ending, you see them die. But no way in hell are they going to bring Toji in as a pilot now. There's too much storyline to cover and with only 1 or 2 Angels left they're not gonna need new Units to fight the Angels. Most of the remaining Eva battles are going to be built around trying to achieve the goals of the various organizations. NERV'S most likely owned Unit 08, SEELE'S Unit 06, and Mari + Kaji's secret agent work in another Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Mar 22, 2010 11:59 pm Who even cares? Toji as a character is boring as hell and these next two movies have way too much shit to go over.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Tue Mar 23, 2010 1:53 am There is also a technical point that might negate this possibility: with the exception of Unit-01, the Evas no longer seem to have this whole mom's soul thing going on. My guess is that Yui Inside is what allowed Shinji to synch up so easily and naturally to Unit-01, but all the other pilots are either supreme entities in human form (Rei and Kaworu), thus allowing them to control an Eva, or have learned to make an Eva move through a long training process (Asuka and probably Mari). In other words, i think that Shinji is the only one who is allowed to do a fall into the cockpit in this continuity, and any other freshly pulled pilot simply wouldn't be able to do jack shit. I guess that is what the ice cream scene in 2.0 alluded to: "You loose" as in, "No plot for you this time".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 23, 2010 2:16 am SaltyJoe wrote: There is also a technical point that might negate this possibility: with the exception of Unit-01, the Evas no longer seem to have this whole mom's soul thing going on.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Tue Mar 23, 2010 3:01 am ^ But wasn't the core taken out way before Asuka was chosen as a pilot for 03? EDIT: Yep, 02 gets frozen at about 54:48, and Asuka is given the call at about 1:00: 15. So i guess that taking out the core is part of the standard deactivation procedure, and they weren't anticipating anything regarding Asuka and 03. Also, when Mari hijacks 02, there is some English and Japanese being spoken about Unit-02 by some computer voice (i guess). It would be nice if someone could make out what is being said, and if they mention 02's core somewhere in there.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 23, 2010 3:15 am SaltyJoe wrote: So i guess that taking out the core is part of the standard deactivation procedure

[]:             Quote Post by Zeruel » Tue Mar 23, 2010 1:24 pm Well then who would they be referring too as "he" when discussing unit 8's pilot???(Unless I'm reading the translation wrong)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 23, 2010 1:29 pm Zeruel wrote: Well then who would they be referring too as "he" when discussing unit 8's pilot???(Unless I'm reading the translation wrong)

[]:             Quote Post by Zeruel » Tue Mar 23, 2010 2:07 pm The pamphlet previewing rebuild 3.0 it was all text.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 23, 2010 2:17 pm Zeruel wrote: The pamphlet previewing rebuild 3.0 it was all text.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeruel » Tue Mar 23, 2010 2:24 pm http://wiki.evageeks.org/Evangelion_2.0:_You_Can_%28Not%29_Advance At the end of the plot summary.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Mar 23, 2010 3:00 pm Reichu wrote: Considering the intimations that Eva-02's core was disembodied so that it could be put into Eva-03, I'm inclined to wait and see what's really going on.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 23, 2010 3:26 pm Zeruel wrote: At the end of the plot summary.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeruel » Tue Mar 23, 2010 3:56 pm Well that makes more sense.
